judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is stronger: both reports match on findings a, b, and c, but only Report 2 clearly states the shared RESULTS_DB path would cause concurrent test runs to collide/corrupt and mandates a private override, whereas Report 1 only hedges ("be aware of the shared default") and downplays the risk.

report_1.evidence: Engineer C — registry (`pipeline/registry.py`): F-02 ... F-05 ... F-09 ... Three trivial edits, but all in one 11-line file — splitting them across people guarantees conflicts, so one person does all three in one commit.

report_2.evidence: Batch B — registry/config (Engineer 2): F-02 (add `aurora-1` to `MODEL_ENDPOINTS`), F-05 ... F-09 ... All three are edits to the same 11-line `pipeline/registry.py`; splitting them across people guarantees conflicts, and together they're small.

report_1.evidence: Ordering: all four streams can start simultaneously — they have zero file overlap. ... Engineer C — registry (`pipeline/registry.py`): F-02 ... F-05 ... F-09 ... one person does all three in one commit. ... Engineer A — batch runner ... F-04 + F-13 ... F-12 (pagination boundary test) ... F-12's test should be written first (red), then the F-04/F-13 fix makes it green.

report_2.evidence: Ordering: because the batches are file-disjoint by construction, all four run as a single parallel wave ... Batch B — registry/config (Engineer 2): F-02 ... F-05 ... F-09 ... All three are edits to the same 11-line `pipeline/registry.py` ... Batch A ... F-12 (pagination boundary tests), F-07 ... F-14 ... F-12 is the regression test for F-04, so it must be the same owner, written test-first

report_1.evidence: F-06 is a calibration decision, not a fix. ... Any number an engineer picks is policy, changes historical comparability of scores, and needs a product/eval-owner sign-off. I'd route it to whoever owns the rubric for concrete values first, then it's a two-line change.

report_2.evidence: This is not a bug fix; it is a scoring-policy change. ... That is a product/eval-owner decision. I would escalate F-06 for a decision on the actual threshold values (without blocking the other batches on the answer), not let an engineer pick numbers.

`tests/conftest.py:4` defaults `RESULTS_DB` to a fixed machine-wide path, `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`. Four engineers (or CI jobs) running pytest concurrently on one machine would share and clobber that database. Each needs a private `RESULTS_DB` value.
